messages.content: write a script about the new conference in 1983 with armstrong atlantic state, augusta state ,campbell, baptist (now charleston southern), coastal carolina, radford, and winthrop

INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - 1983

Several university representatives are seated around a large conference table. The room is lined with banners and posters showcasing the various schools.

JOHN, the commissioner of the conference, stands at the front of the room.

JOHN:
Good morning, everyone. I'd like to thank each of you for coming today. As you know, we are here to discuss the formation of a new athletic conference.

The room murmurs with excitement.

JOHN:
We have representatives from Armstrong Atlantic State, Augusta State, Campbell, Baptist (now Charleston Southern), Coastal Carolina, Radford, and Winthrop.

Each representative nods in acknowledgement.

JOHN:
Together, we have the potential to form a dynamic and competitive conference. We will have the opportunity to showcase our universities, compete on a more level playing field, and provide our students with a top-notch athletic experience.

The room erupts in applause.

JOHN:
Now, let's get down to business. We'll need to discuss the overall structure of the conference, scheduling, and of course, funding.

The representatives dive into discussions and debates. They discuss facility requirements, travel allowances, and divisional options.

After several hours of discussion, the representatives finally come to an agreement. A collective vision has been made.

JOHN:
I'm pleased to announce that an agreement has been reached. We have formed the Big South Conference!

The room erupts in cheers and applause.

JOHN:
Thank you again to each of the universities for your dedication and hard work. Together, we will create a powerhouse of a conference and pave the way for generations of student-athletes to come.

The representatives shake hands and exchange congratulations as they exit the conference room, excited for what the future holds for the Big South Conference.

INT. COLUMBUS STATE UNIVERSITY - 1983

A group of coaches and administrators from Columbus State University are gathered in a conference room. They are discussing the possibility of joining the newly formed Big South Conference.

COACH:
We've been talking to Commissioner John about joining the conference, and it looks like we're very close to making it happen.

ADMINISTRATOR:
We've always wanted to elevate our athletic program, and joining the Big South could be the perfect opportunity for us.

However, not everyone in the room seems convinced.

COACH 2:
I'm not so sure about this. We have to consider the travel expenses and time commitments that come with joining a new conference. It could end up being a significant burden for our student-athletes.

Another coach speaks up.

COACH 3:
But on the other hand, being part of a bigger conference could attract more talented athletes to our university, which would elevate our program and give us more chances for success.

The group continues to debate, weighing the pros and cons of joining the Big South Conference.

Finally, the administrator speaks up.

ADMINISTRATOR:
After hearing everyone's thoughts on this, I say we take the plunge and join the Big South Conference. We have a lot to gain, and we'll figure out the challenges as we go along.

The room nods in agreement. A decision has been made.

INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - 1984

The Big South Conference is holding its annual meeting. Columbus State University representatives are present as they hope to become a member.

JOHN:
As we move forward with the growth of our conference, we have had discussions with Columbus State University about possible membership. Would the representatives from Columbus State like to address the conference?

The Columbus State representatives stand.

COLUMBUS STATE REP:
Thank you, Commissioner John. We are thrilled to be able to have the opportunity to join this exciting and competitive conference. We hope to bring a lot to the table and contribute to the growth and success of the Big South.

The representatives from the other universities nod and offer congratulations.

JOHN:
We will continue discussions with Columbus State and hope to have them as a new member of the Big South Conference in the near future.

Columbus State University would ultimately not join the conference at this time, but the possibility was still open for the future. The Big South Conference would go on to become a successful and competitive conference, with Columbus State watching from the sidelines, eager to join.

INT. UNC ASHEVILLE - 1984

A group of coaches and administrators at UNC Asheville are gathered in a conference room. They are discussing the possibility of joining the Big South Conference.

COACH:
I've been in talks with Commissioner John about joining the conference, and they seem interested in having us.

ADMINISTRATOR:
Joining the Big South would give us more opportunities to compete at a higher level, and potentially attract more talented student-athletes to our university.

However, not everyone in the room is convinced.

COACH 2:
What about the travel expenses and time commitments? We don't want to overburden our student-athletes with too much time on the road.

Another coach speaks up.

COACH 3:
But on the other hand, being part of a bigger conference could help elevate our program and give us more chances for success. We can make it work.

The group continues to debate, weighing the pros and cons of joining the Big South Conference.

Finally, the administrator speaks up.

ADMINISTRATOR:
After hearing everyone's thoughts on this, I say we take the opportunity and join the Big South Conference. We have a lot to gain, and we can make sure we manage any challenges that come with it.

The room nods in agreement. UNC Asheville will join the Big South Conference.

INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - 1984

The Big South Conference is holding its annual meeting. UNC Asheville representatives are present as they hope to become a member.

JOHN:
As we move forward with the growth of our conference, we have had discussions with UNC Asheville about possible membership. Would the representatives from UNC Asheville like to address the conference?

The UNC Asheville representatives stand.

UNC ASHEVILLE REP:
Thank you, Commissioner John. We are excited to be invited to the Big South Conference and we look forward to contributing to its success. We believe this is an excellent opportunity for our university and our student-athletes.

The representatives from the other universities nod and welcome UNC Asheville to the Big South Conference.

JOHN:
We are thrilled to add UNC Asheville to the Big South Conference family. We look forward to working together and creating more opportunities for all our universities.

UNC Asheville would go on to become a respected member of the Big South Conference and would achieve success in various sports, eventually winning the Big South Championship in men's basketball in 2011.
armstrong state drops out of division 1 1987, stating financially not being able to be in division 1

INT. ARMSTRONG STATE UNIVERSITY - 1987

A group of coaches and administrators at Armstrong State University are gathered in a conference room. They are discussing the financial challenges that have arisen regarding their participation in Division 1 of the NCAA.

COACH:
Our expenses have been increasing exponentially over recent years, and we are no longer able to maintain our position in Division 1.

ADMINISTRATOR:
We need to consider the long-term financial implications of staying in Division 1. We may have to let go of some of our sports programs or reduce scholarships to maintain our position, which would ultimately hurt our athletic program.

The group continues to debate, weighing the pros and cons of Armstrong State University's participation in NCAA Division 1.

Finally, the administrator speaks up.


ADMINISTRATOR:
After reviewing our financial situation, we've decided that the best course of action is to drop down from Division 1.

The room falls silent.

COACH 2:
What about the athletes that have committed to our university? How will this impact them?

ADMINISTRATOR:
We will continue to honor all of our commitments to our student-athletes, and work to ensure they have the best experience possible.

COACH 3:
What about our fans? How will this affect them?

ADMINISTRATOR:
We understand that fans may be disappointed, but we need to do what’s best for our university and our athletic programs. We hope that they will continue to support us as we move forward as a Division 2 member.

INT. NCAA CONFERENCE ROOM - 1987

Armstrong State University representatives attend an NCAA Division 2 membership application meeting.

ARMSTRONG STATE REP:
Thank you for considering our application for membership in Division 2. We've made the decision to move down from Division 1 due to financial challenges, but we believe that moving forward, our athletic programs will continue to be competitive and successful.

The NCAA members nod and congratulate Armstrong State on their decision.

JOHN:
We accept Armstrong State University as a new member of NCAA Division 2.

Armstrong State University would move forward as a respected member of Division 2, winning conference championships in various sports such as tennis, softball, and soccer. The university would continue its dedication to providing its student-athletes with a top athletic experience while maintaining its financial stability in the long term.
augusta state also drops out of division 1 for the same reasons 1990 davidson replaces them

INT. AUGUSTA STATE UNIVERSITY - 1990

A group of coaches and administrators at Augusta State University are gathered in a conference room. They are discussing the financial challenges that have arisen regarding their participation in Division 1 of the NCAA.

COACH:
Our expenses have been increasing exponentially over recent years, and we are no longer able to maintain our position in Division 1.

ADMINISTRATOR:
We need to consider the long-term financial implications of staying in Division 1. We may have to let go of some of our sports programs or reduce scholarships to maintain our position, which would ultimately hurt our athletic program.

The group continues to debate, weighing the pros and cons of Augusta State University's participation in NCAA Division 1.

Finally, the administrator speaks up.

ADMINISTRATOR:
After reviewing our financial situation, we've decided that the best course of action is to drop down from Division 1.

The room falls silent.

COACH 2:
What about the athletes that have committed to our university? How will this impact them?

ADMINISTRATOR:
We will continue to honor all of our commitments to our student-athletes, and work to ensure they have the best experience possible.

COACH 3:
What about our fans? How will this affect them?

ADMINISTRATOR:
We understand that fans may be disappointed, but we need to do what’s best for our university and our athletic programs. We hope that they will continue to support us as we move forward as a Division 2 member.

INT. NCAA CONFERENCE ROOM - 1990

Augusta State University representatives attend an NCAA Division 2 membership application meeting.

AUGUSTA STATE REP:
Thank you for considering our application for membership in Division 2. We've made the decision to move down from Division 1 due to financial challenges, but we believe that moving forward, our athletic programs will continue to be competitive and successful.

The NCAA members nod and congratulate Augusta State on their decision.

JOHN:
We accept Augusta State University as a new member of NCAA Division 2.

Augusta State University would move forward as a respected member of Division 2, winning conference championships in various sports such as golf, soccer, and volleyball. However, the Big South Conference would find a replacement for Augusta State University, as the conference would welcome Davidson College in their place. Davidson College, formerly a member of the Southern Conference, would bring a new level of competition and success to the Big South Conference.

INT. LIBERTY UNIVERSITY - 1991

A group of coaches and administrators at Liberty University are gathered in a conference room, discussing the possibility of joining the Big South Conference.

COACH:
We've been talking to Commissioner John about joining the conference, and it looks like we're very close to making it happen.

ADMINISTRATOR:
Joining the Big South would be a great opportunity for our athletic program. We would be able to compete at a higher level and showcase our university on a bigger stage.

However, not everyone in the room seems convinced.

COACH 2:
What about the travel expenses and time commitments that come with joining a new conference? It could end up being a significant burden for our student-athletes.

Another coach speaks up.

COACH 3:
But on the other hand, being part of a bigger conference would attract more talented athletes to our university, and give us more opportunities for success. We can make it work.

The group continues to debate, weighing the pros and cons of joining the Big South Conference.

Finally, the administrator speaks up.

ADMINISTRATOR:
After considering the potential benefits and challenges, I say we take the opportunity and join the Big South Conference. We have a lot to gain, and we can manage any challenges that come with it.

The room nods in agreement. Liberty University will join the Big South Conference.

INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - 1991

The Big South Conference is holding its annual meeting. Liberty University representatives are present, hoping to become a member.

JOHN:
As we move forward with the growth of our conference, we have had discussions with Liberty University about possible membership. Would the representatives from Liberty University like to address the conference?

The Liberty University representatives stand.

LIBERTY REP:
Thank you, Commissioner John. We are excited to have the opportunity to join the Big South Conference. We believe this is an excellent opportunity for our university and our student-athletes, and we look forward to contributing to its success.

The representatives from the other universities nod and offer congratulations.

JOHN:
We welcome Liberty University to the Big South Conference. We look forward to working together and creating more opportunities for all our universities.

Liberty University would go on to become a successful and competitive member of the Big South Conference, achieving success in various sports such as football, basketball, and baseball. The university would also go on to win multiple conference championships, including in football in 2008 and 2019.
davidson to socon 1992 replacements are towson uncg and umbc

INT. BIG SOUTH CONFERENCE ROOM - 1992

Representatives from the Big South Conference are gathered in a conference room, including Commissioner John and several university officials. They are discussing the news that Davidson College will be leaving the conference to join the Southern Conference.

JOHN:
It's unfortunate to lose Davidson from our conference, but we wish them all the best in the Southern Conference.

An official from Towson University speaks up.

TOWSON OFFICIAL:
We have heard of the opening in the Big South Conference, and we are interested in joining.

Other university officials express interest, including those from UNC Greensboro and University of Maryland, Baltimore County (UMBC).

JOHN:
Thank you all for expressing interest in joining the Big South Conference. We will review your applications and get back to you as soon as possible.

After a review process, Towson University, UNC Greensboro, and UMBC are all accepted as members of the Big South Conference.

JOHN:
We welcome Towson, UNC Greensboro, and UMBC as new members of the Big South Conference. We believe that these three universities will add to the depth and competitiveness of our conference.

The university officials nod in agreement and congratulate the new members.

TOWSON OFFICIAL:
We are thrilled to be part of the Big South Conference and look forward to competing against the other universities in our new conference.

UNC GREENSBORO OFFICIAL:
We are excited to have the opportunity to join such a competitive conference, and we hope to make meaningful contributions to its success.

UMBC OFFICIAL:
We believe that we will be able to compete at a high level in the Big South Conference and we look forward to building relationships with the other member universities.

The Big South Conference moves forward with the new members, continuing to showcase competitive athletic programs and providing an excellent experience for student-athletes.
sunday play begins and campbell leaves for taac 1994

INT. BIG SOUTH CONFERENCE ROOM - 1994

Representatives from the Big South Conference are gathered in a conference room, including Commissioner John and several university officials. They are discussing two significant changes in the conference.

JOHN:
First of all, I'm happy to announce that Sunday play will now be allowed in the Big South Conference. This is a significant step forward for our conference, and we hope it will lead to more exposure and opportunities for our student-athletes.

The representatives nod in agreement and congratulate Commissioner John on the achievement.

JOHN:
However, I also have some unfortunate news to share. Campbell University has decided to leave the Big South Conference and join the TAAC.

The room falls silent as the representatives absorb the news.

CAMPBELL OFFICIAL:
We have enjoyed our time in the Big South Conference, but we believe that joining the TAAC will be the best move for our university and our athletic program.

The Big South Conference officials thank Campbell for their time in the conference and wish them all the best in the TAAC.

JOHN:
We will begin the process of finding a replacement for Campbell in the conference, and we are committed to continuing to build and grow the Big South Conference.

The representatives nod in agreement and commend Commissioner John for his leadership.

In the following months, the Big South Conference finds a replacement for Campbell. Radford University joins the conference, bringing a new level of competition and success.

The Big South Conference continues to thrive, providing student-athletes with excellent athletic experiences and continuing to push for growth and development in the world of sports.
towson to north atlantic (now the america east) 1995

INT. BIG SOUTH CONFERENCE ROOM - 1995

Representatives from the Big South Conference are gathered in a conference room, including Commissioner John and several university officials. They are discussing the news that Towson University will be leaving the conference to join the North Atlantic Conference (now America East).

JOHN:
We have been notified that Towson University will be leaving the Big South Conference to join the North Atlantic Conference. This is disappointing news, but we wish Towson all the best in their new conference.

TOWSON OFFICIAL:
We have enjoyed our time in the Big South Conference, but we believe that joining the North Atlantic Conference will be better for our university and our athletic program.

The representatives nod in agreement and commend Towson for their time in the conference.

JOHN:
We will begin the process of finding a replacement for Towson in the conference, and we are committed to continuing to build and grow the Big South Conference.

After a review process, the Big South Conference accepts Birmingham-Southern College as a member of the conference.

JOHN:
We welcome Birmingham-Southern College as a new member of the Big South Conference. We believe that they will add to the depth and competitiveness of our conference.

The university officials nod in agreement and congratulate Birmingham-Southern College on their acceptance.

BIRMINGHAM SOUTHERN OFFICIAL:
We are thrilled to be part of the Big South Conference and look forward to competing against the other universities in our new conference.

The Big South Conference moves forward with the new member, continuing to showcase competitive athletic programs and providing an excellent experience for student-athletes. The conference remains committed to growth and development within the world of sports.
